subject: Mozi Minute: LTV:CAC Reality Check
body:
Words I like: Your ability to tolerate boredom while repeating the same action determines your odds of success.

Mozi Minute: LTV:CAC Reality Check

Most founders chase the magic 3:1 LTV:CAC ratio like it's a rule. I've found that's wrong to do for most real businesses.

That 3:1 standard only works if you've built an automated machine across...

- Getting leads (Paid Ads VS Manual Outreach),

- Converting leads (checkout page VS 1-1 salesmen),

- and delivering to them (software/physical products VS service).

And fun fact - and most of us haven't. The less automated each step, the higher that ratio needs to be.

Here's the real ratio you need to scale based on what I've found:

- If all three parts run themselves (getting leads + selling + fulfillment): 3:1 works

- Two parts run themselves: you need 6:1 at least

- One part runs itself: aim for 9:1

- Nothing runs itself (all steps involve people): you need at least 12:1+

→ And remember, these are minimums, not goals.

I like to be much higher than this.

Why? Because when you grow, things break:

- New hires don't work as well as your best people

- You have to find leads in harder spots

- Your systems start to strain

- Infrastructure costs money
- Leads take longer to buy
- Work gets harder to do right

Bottom line: the more people have to do the work in your business, the more money you need to make on each sale to grow safely.

This is my rule of thumb. Score your business and see how you do.

As promised, value in under a minute.

Save the cheerleader, save the world.

Alex
